subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Try Again
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: A small boy named Sam was trying to fly a kite in the park. The wind was strong, and every time the kite went up, it crashed back down. Sam felt like giving up because his hands were tired.
questions: Who is the main character?
What was Sam trying to do?
Where was he?
How was the wind?
Why did he feel like giving up?

passages.paragraph: His grandfather sat on a bench nearby and watched him. He smiled and told Sam that failure is just a lesson. He said, 'If at first you don't succeed, try, try again.'
questions: Who was watching Sam?
Where was the grandfather sitting?
What did the grandfather do when he spoke?
What is failure, according to the grandfather?
What famous advice did the grandfather give?

passages.paragraph: Sam looked at his broken kite and sighed. He thought about his grandfather's words. He decided to fix the paper wings of the kite with some tape he had in his pocket.
questions: What was the condition of the kite?
What did Sam do before thinking?
Whose words did he think about?
What part of the kite did he fix?
What did he use to fix the kite?

passages.paragraph: The second time Sam tried, the kite flew a little higher. But suddenly, it got stuck in the branches of a tall oak tree. Sam ran to the tree and looked up at the leaves.
questions: How did the kite fly the second time?
What happened to the kite suddenly?
What kind of tree was it?
What did Sam do after the kite got stuck?
Where did Sam look?

passages.paragraph: Sam used a long stick to poke the kite. It took many tries, but finally, the kite fell softly onto the grass. He was happy he did not leave it behind in the tree.
questions: What tool did Sam use to get the kite?
Did it work on the first try?
Where did the kite fall?
How did the kite fall?
Why was Sam happy?

passages.paragraph: The sun was starting to set, but Sam wanted to try one more time. He ran across the field as fast as his legs could carry him. He held the string tightly in his hand.
questions: What was happening to the sun?
How many more times did Sam want to try?
Where did he run?
How fast did he run?
What was he holding tightly?

passages.paragraph: Suddenly, a steady breeze caught the kite. It rose above the trees and into the orange sky. Sam cheered loudly as he watched the kite dance in the air.
questions: What caught the kite suddenly?
Where did the kite rise?
What color was the sky?
What did Sam do when the kite flew?
What was the kite doing in the air?

passages.paragraph: His grandfather walked over and patted him on the back. He told Sam that persistence is the key to success. Sam realized that failing the first time didn't mean he was bad at flying kites.
questions: What did the grandfather do to encourage Sam?
What is the 'key to success' according to the text?
What did Sam realize about failing?
Did failing mean he was bad at flying kites?
Who walked over to Sam?

passages.paragraph: Sam learned that even if something is hard, you should keep going. He felt proud of himself for not quitting when the kite crashed or got stuck in the tree.
questions: What should you do if something is hard?
How did Sam feel about himself?
What were the two times Sam could have quit?
Did Sam quit in the end?
What lesson did Sam learn?

passages.paragraph: The lesson of 'Try Again' is important for everyone. It means that with patience and hard work, we can reach our goals. Sam went home that day with a big smile on his face.
questions: Is the lesson important for everyone?
What two things help us reach our goals?
What does the lesson mean?
Where did Sam go at the end of the day?
How did Sam look as he went home?
